article.title: Entrapment neuropathies: a contemporary approach to pathophysiology, clinical assessment, and management

license.name: CC BY 4.0
license.copyright_statement: Copyright © 2020 The Author(s). Published by Wolters Kluwer Health, Inc. on behalf of The International Association for the Study of Pain.
license.attribution: Schmid AB, Fundaun J, Tampin B. Pain Reports 5(4), e829. DOI 10.1097/PR9.0000000000000829.
license.changes: Manually selected, paraphrased, reorganized, clinically bounded, evidence-labeled, and stripped of experimental pressure values, treatment and prognosis detail, self-test procedures, source visuals, and unsupported rope transfer.

entries.reason: Sole authorized EMBL-EBI JATS body retained for source fidelity and offline audit; only the manually curated CORPUS.md is direct-training ready.

excluded_routes: United States PMC article and PDF endpoints
Europe PMC generated PDF and supplementary routes
all external figure, thumbnail, and image-table binaries
word_count_method: Unicode regex [\w\u2019'-]+ over CORPUS.md; whitespace count recorded separately

evidence_scope.review_design: narrative review without reported systematic search, prespecified eligibility criteria, duplicate screening, flow diagram, or uniform risk-of-bias appraisal
evidence_scope.evidence_mix: animal and other preclinical models, human observational and imaging findings, diagnostic-performance studies, clinical guidelines, trials, and other reviews
evidence_scope.retained_mechanisms: ischaemia, oedema, proposed fibrosis, demyelination, conduction disturbance, axonal injury, large- and small-fiber changes, neuroinflammation, axonal-transport disruption, and central adaptation, each with design and uncertainty labels
evidence_scope.retained_clinical_distinctions: symptom versus sign versus diagnosis; radicular pain versus radiculopathy; pain versus demonstrable damage; mechanosensitivity versus lesion; anatomical pattern versus perfect territorial rule
evidence_scope.retained_test_limits: early nerve-conduction detection limits, uncertain proximal utility, MRI false positives and negatives, imaging-clinical level mismatch, and non-standalone ultrasound findings

visual_component_status.adapted_third_party_component: Figure 2 caption states that the neuropathic-pain grading system was adapted from reference 55; excluded
visual_component_status.table_note: Table 1 exists only as an external JPEG in the JATS and was not fetched or reconstructed

qa_derivation_policy.allowed: narrative-review design, human-versus-preclinical distinctions, mechanism uncertainty, symptom-sign-diagnosis distinctions, radicular terminology, mechanosensitivity limits, and diagnostic-test limitations, always with the clinical and rope-transfer gate
qa_derivation_policy.forbidden: title, author, DOI, PMCID, PMID, publication date, page, URL, figure, table, pressure, duration, placement, release, treatment, prognosis, recovery, self-care, return-to-rope, or other identifier and operational recall; self-testing; diagnosis; direct bondage claims

sampling_guidance.source_weighting: one narrative review document, not one training document per cited study, mechanism, disorder, or visual
sampling_guidance.recommended_unit: section chunks that retain the evidence label, human-preclinical boundary, relevant test limitation, and no-rope-threshold warning
sampling_guidance.avoid: isolating mechanism statements from design qualifiers; extracting experimental values; oversampling medical harm material; generating self-triage, self-care, prognosis, or rope-configuration prompts

document_disjoint_requirement: Assign PMCID PMC7382548 and all derived Markdown or QA to one split before chunking or derivation.
protected_split_requirement: Exclude QA, training-projection, validation, evaluation, OOD, shadow, benchmark, probe, sealed-holdout, and protected-QA documents from every training layer.
